rationale: Shares 'off platform'/'off-platform' with 'Going off app' (Diversion). Keep those keywords in 'Going off app' as the canonical leaving-the-platform concept; replace in 'Off-platform management' with management-specific phrases.
'cancellation policy' is the literal name of 'Cancellation policies' (Recurring billings). Removing it from Business 'Cancellations' eliminates dual firing.
Bare 'lead time' fires on both Long and Short lead time discussions. Specialize the keyword for Long.

proposed: rename 'Recurring bookings' to 'Recurring rates' and narrow keywords to rate-setting phrases ('recurring discount', 'recurring rate'); otherwise merge into Recurring billings theme
rationale: 'Recurring bookings' (Preferences and rates) overlaps conceptually with the entire 'Recurring billings' theme. A post about modifying a weekly schedule could fire on both.
Bare 'glitch' and 'bug' overlap with 'Glitches and bugs (Cards)'. Adding 'app' qualifier narrows the general bucket and lets card-specific keywords fire alone for card-context posts.
Conceptual overlap with 'Paying upfront' on the word 'upfront'. Re-scope Pricing transparency to visibility of total cost; keep payment-timing language in Paying upfront.
Bare 'stars' is generic and clashes with 'Star Sitter' problem. Specialize to '5 stars'.

proposed: split into specific problems: Message reactions, Search inbox, Timezone display, etc.
rationale: 'Functionality' is too broad to be useful for analytics. Splitting into named features eliminates ambiguity with 'Reminders' and gives the dashboard meaningful trends.

proposed: Centralize: add 'in-app crm', 'native client list'. Off-platform management: add 'spreadsheet', 'external tool'.
rationale: Both deal with managing clients/relationships. Sharper definitions: Centralize = wanting Rover-native CRM; Off-platform management = already managing externally and wanting Rover support for that.

proposed: Capacity comprehension: focus on numeric dog count limits. Dog sizes: focus on size-tier preferences and weight categories.
rationale: Posts about 'how many big dogs' fire both. Sharpen the boundary.
'Covered' is extremely common in unrelated contexts: 'dog covered in mud', 'I got covered for the holiday'. Massive false-positive risk.
'Protect' fires on 'protect my dog from heat', 'protect against fleas', 'protect my profile'. Far too generic.
'Liability' alone overlaps with 'liability waiver' from Contracts.
Extremely common English phrase ('the dog doesn't bother me') unrelated to working-hours boundaries.
'Data' is too generic — fires on 'mobile data', 'data plan', 'no data signal'.
Bare 'ranking' fires on 'star ranking', 'breed ranking', 'ranking my favorite parks'.
'Review' fires on 'I review the listing', 'reviewing my schedule', 'product review', 'app review'.
Bare 'rating' is generic — 'movie rating', 'rating the breed'.
'Slow' fires on 'slow dog', 'slow walker', 'slow app', 'slow payout', 'slow to respond'.
'Report' is highly ambiguous: 'report a user', 'report an issue', 'incident report' — none about earnings/business reports.
'Summary' is generic — 'profile summary', 'in summary', 'summary of the post' all fire unrelated to financial reports.
'Profile' is one of the most common words in a sitter forum — 'her profile said X', 'check the owner's profile', 'profile picture'.
Bare 'cancel' fires on every flavor of cancellation including ones that belong in other problems.
'Broken' fires on 'broken leash', 'broken bone', 'broken record', 'broken trust'.
'Error' fires on 'human error', 'error in judgment'.
'Lag' is short and ambiguous (jet lag).
'Freeze' fires on freezing weather walks, 'freeze frame', non-app contexts.
'Crash' fires on 'car crash', 'crash on my couch', 'crash course'.
'Not working' fires on 'the dog is not working with me', 'my methods are not working'.
Generic descriptor — 'owner's instructions were confusing', 'this thread is confusing'.
'Navigate' fires on 'navigate the conversation', 'navigate the relationship'.
Generic adjective — 'complicated client', 'complicated dog'.
'Tip' is also advice — 'a tip for new sitters', 'tip: bring a leash'. Fires on every tips-and-tricks post.
'Deal' has many uses — 'deal with the dog', 'a big deal', 'deal-breaker'.
Doesn't distinguish cats-as-rate-preference from any cat-related post.
'Trial' fires on 'free trial', 'trial period', 'trial and error', and Rover Care 365 trials.
'Tracking' is generic — 'tracking my expenses', 'tracking the package'.
Common in pet-sitting context unrelated to self-promotion: 'the neighborhood dog', 'neighborhood walks'.
Sitters discuss owners' social media posts, social media of pets — not necessarily self-promotion.
Overlaps with 'Expenses' which uses 'reimburse expense'. Bare 'reimburse' fires on both.
Extremely generic — 'pick up after the dog', 'pick up the leash'.
'Drop off' fires on 'drop off in energy', 'drop off in bookings'.
'Offline' is broadly used: 'taking the conversation offline', 'I'm going offline'.
Bare 'christmas' fires on any Christmas mention (gifts, day walks).
'Just started' is generic — 'just started a new puppy training class', 'just started with this client'.
Different concept from autocorrect-during-typing; rarely appears in r/RoverPetSitting context.
Highly generic — 'I'm unavailable that day', 'sitter is unavailable'.
Fires on 'owner on vacation' which is the reason for the booking, not the sitter's time-off concept.
Sitters discuss client/owner timezone differences for travel, not just an app feature.
'Commission' could appear in non-Rover contexts (sales commission).
Rigid phrasing; people phrase this many ways. Lower-confidence flag.
Frequent and emotional topic on r/RoverPetSitting — sitters being suspended, deactivated, or having accounts on hold. No current problem captures platform-initiated termination.
Most frequent recurring complaint on the subreddit is the quality and responsiveness of Rover Support.
Sitters frequently post about dog bites, dog fights, aggressive dogs, and feeling unsafe. 'Breeds' covers breed-related concerns but not the broader incident category.
Posts about a sitter losing a pet, a pet escaping, or a pet getting injured during a stay are common and emotionally heavy.
Heavy content area — entitled owners, rude messages, unrealistic demands. 'Dropping clients' is the action; this captures the underlying problem of bad client behavior.
Recurring theme: exhaustion, burnout, second-guessing being a sitter, considering quitting.
Frequent posts about dirty homes, no clean bedding, no food left, broken AC, similar housing issues for house-sitters.
Push notification volume, missed notifications for new requests, and notification settings are recurring pain points. 'Reminders' is adjacent but specific.
Posts about scam owners, no-show clients, fake profiles, and verifying client identity are common.
Sitters frequently ask about auto-declining incoming requests, declining without penalty, and how declines affect search rank.
Sitters frequently complain about owners not providing care instructions or providing contradictory info. 'Intake form' is the requested feature; this is the underlying complaint.
Sitters often write about limiting which services they offer. 'Service structure' is generic; this is a specific recurring distinction.
Sitters discuss competing sitters in their area, undercutting, oversaturation. 'Low demand' is adjacent.
Common complaints about owners demanding constant photo updates. 'Live updates' covers feature requests; this is the cultural/expectation pain point.
Sitters post about owners disputing charges. Distinct from refunds and recurring-billing payment issues.
